mission.title: Install and Configure Submariner on Kubernetes
mission.description: Submariner connects overlay networks of different Kubernetes clusters, enabling direct networking and service discovery across clusters. It is CNI-agnostic and supports both encrypted and non-encrypted tunnels between connected clusters.

steps.title: Install the subctl CLI
steps.description:
Download and install the subctl CLI, which simplifies deployment and maintenance of Submariner.
```bash
curl -Ls https://get.submariner.io | VERSION=v0.23.1 bash
sudo mv ~/.local/bin/subctl /usr/local/bin/
subctl version
```

steps.title: Deploy the Submariner Broker
steps.description:
Deploy the Broker on one of your clusters. The Broker facilitates the exchange of metadata between connected clusters.
```bash
subctl deploy-broker --kubeconfig /path/to/broker-cluster/kubeconfig
```

steps.title: Join clusters to the Broker
steps.description:
Join each participating cluster to the Broker. This installs the Submariner components on the cluster.
```bash
subctl join broker-info.subm --kubeconfig /path/to/cluster-a/kubeconfig --clusterid cluster-a
subctl join broker-info.subm --kubeconfig /path/to/cluster-b/kubeconfig --clusterid cluster-b
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Use subctl to verify the Submariner deployment is healthy.
```bash
subctl show all --kubeconfig /path/to/cluster-a/kubeconfig
subctl verify --kubeconfig /path/to/cluster-a/kubeconfig --toconfig /path/to/cluster-b/kubeconfig
```

steps.title: Test cross-cluster connectivity
steps.description:
Deploy a test service on one cluster and access it from another cluster.
```bash
# On cluster-a
kubectl create deployment nginx --image=nginx
kubectl expose deployment nginx --port=80
subctl export service nginx --namespace default

# On cluster-b
kubectl run test --image=busybox --command -- wget -qO- nginx.default.svc.clusterset.local
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have Submariner components running on all joined clusters with secure tunnels established between gateway nodes. Services can be exported and accessed across clusters using the .clusterset.local DNS domain.

uninstall.title: Disconnect clusters
uninstall.description: Use subctl to remove Submariner from each cluster.

uninstall.title: Remove the Broker
uninstall.description: Delete the Submariner Broker resources from the broker cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade subctl
upgrade.description: Download the latest version of subctl using the install script.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Submariner on clusters
upgrade.description: Use the upgraded subctl to upgrade Submariner components on each cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Gateway node not elected
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure at least one node has the gateway.submariner.io/public-ip label and UDP ports 4500 and 4490 are open.
```bash
kubectl get nodes -l submariner.io/gateway=true
```

troubleshooting.title: Cross-cluster connectivity fails
troubleshooting.description:
Run the subctl diagnose commands to identify issues.
```bash
subctl diagnose all
```

troubleshooting.title: Service discovery not working
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the Lighthouse components are running and services are exported.
```bash
kubectl get serviceexport
subctl show connections
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have at least two Kubernetes clusters running version 1.24 or higher with non-overlapping Pod and Service CIDRs. Gateway nodes need UDP ports 4500 and 4490 open.
